method.type: post-hoc qualitative semantic and route-contract audit
method.coverage: Every mechanically accepted observation whose postprocessing word-error distance exceeded its raw-ASR distance was reviewed against the frozen reference, raw input, and model output. Every terminal invalid response was also included in the route decision.
method.boundary: The audit does not rewrite the precommitted expected-utility gates. It distinguishes reference-tokenization artifacts and benign grammar from meaning changes, then produces a product-shortlist recommendation.
limitations: The review was model-assisted and was not independently adjudicated by native speakers.
The synthetic source deliberately contains some unusual wording. A postprocessor must preserve what was synthesized rather than silently replace it with a more plausible fact.
The first Claude terminal response was recorded retroactively after the earlier runner discarded its HTTP-200 envelope; its response digest, duration, usage, and cost are unavailable.
The selected development slice is useful for comparative screening but is not a release-quality estimate.

candidate_findings.finding: Sol provides the largest aggregate WER reduction, but it repeatedly rewrites an English present-tense statement into past tense and reconstructs an unusual Spanish date range into a more plausible period. The other three unique WER regressions are meaning-preserving spelling, tokenization, or parenthetical repairs.
candidate_findings.operational_recommendation: Retain as the hosted quality ceiling, not the unattended default. Protected-span handling for dates, numbers, names, negation, and tense-bearing verbs should precede product enablement.

candidate_findings.finding: DeepSeek delivers roughly one fifth aggregate WER reduction at negligible cost and never changes a correct target into an error. One dialogue changes speaker perspective from `you` to `we`; two repeated WER increases are meaning-preserving range or grammar repairs, and one Spanish dialogue remains locally unstable while mostly cleaning a noisy input.
candidate_findings.operational_recommendation: Retain as the primary value candidate and the strongest new challenger. It is the best next implementation target after protected-span and dialogue-speaker safeguards.

candidate_findings.finding: MiniMax remains net positive, but each WER regression contains a material reconstruction: a changed date plus unsupported phrase, a changed Spanish clause subject, or invented French dialogue content.
candidate_findings.operational_recommendation: Keep the benchmark evidence but do not add MiniMax to the product shortlist. DeepSeek is both cheaper and materially stronger on this slice.

candidate_findings.finding: Claude has a strong and clean accepted-output quality signal, but three of the first 25 repeat-two logical requests returned HTTP 200 without usable text. The pinned Azure route therefore made the 118-of-120 mechanical minimum mathematically unreachable.
candidate_findings.operational_recommendation: Do not expose this pinned route. A different ZDR provider may be screened later if Claude remains strategically important, without changing the model-quality interpretation of the accepted outputs.

reviewed_cases.assessment: Changes Lea's question from `Why do you leave` to `Why do we leave`, shifting speaker perspective without evidence.
Renders the spoken price interval as `$75 to $125`; the reference uses a dash that WER tokenizes differently.
Cleans punctuation and several obvious ASR fragments but retains an unstable speaker-name and clause reconstruction.
Uses the idiomatic `o mais aproximada possível` construction and repairs sentence punctuation without changing the claim.
Changes the synthesized 1981 wording to 1980 and inserts `según lo visto`, which has no acoustic support.
Repairs punctuation but changes the final statement from humans solving problems intuitively to early AI research doing the solving.
Repairs several obvious fragments but invents `il n'y a rien à y faire` and makes unsupported speaker/name substitutions.
Changes `become` and `grow` to `became` and `grew` in an already exact target in both repeats.
Rewrites `out loud` as `aloud` and normalizes compound presentation without changing meaning.
Repairs the quoted phrase but changes `become` and `grow` to past tense without acoustic support.
Normalizes `micro worlds`, `common sense`, and compound hyphenation; WER penalizes the presentation change.
Repairs the garbled discourse fragment but rewrites the unusual synthesized date range into the more plausible late 1980s and early 1990s.
Reconnects the `ledor` definition and repairs the Dorina Nowill name; the singular/plural wording causes the numeric WER increase without changing the definition.

not_shortlisted.reason: Lower aggregate gain than DeepSeek with three material regression cases.
Pinned route failed the mechanical response contract despite strong accepted-output quality.

shortlist_decision.product_boundary: No candidate becomes an unconditional default from this development screen. Raw transcript preservation, optional enhancement, protected fact-bearing spans, and later held-out professional-audio evidence remain required.
